archetype: Automated Underwater Archaeological Site Survey and Excavation Planning System
description: This system aids underwater archaeologists in surveying and excavating submerged sites by processing sonar and sub-bottom profiling data to identify potential artifacts, generate 3D models of the site, and plan excavation routes. It's used by research institutions and government agencies to preserve cultural heritage. The system's technical challenges include handling large datasets, integrating multiple sensor inputs, and ensuring accuracy in low-visibility environments.
rules: MUST integrate with side-scan sonar and sub-bottom profiling equipment to collect site data
MUST adhere to UNESCO's Convention on the Protection of the Underwater Cultural Heritage
MUST support 3D modeling and visualization of the site to aid in excavation planning
MUST incorporate machine learning algorithms to identify potential artifacts in the sonar data
MUST ensure data integrity and security to protect sensitive cultural and historical information
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely solely on manual data analysis, as it is time-consuming and prone to human error
DO NOT use a generic GIS platform without customization for underwater archaeology
DO NOT neglect to account for water conditions and their impact on sensor data accuracy
key_technical_challenges: Integrating diverse sensor data types and formats
Developing accurate machine learning models for artifact identification
Optimizing 3D modeling and visualization for low-visibility environments

recommended_stack.language: Python, due to its extensive libraries for scientific computing and machine learning
recommended_stack.framework: Django, for its robust support of data-driven applications
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL, for its ability to handle spatial data and large datasets
recommended_stack.cache: Redis, for its performance in handling real-time data
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka, for its reliability in handling large volumes of sensor data

compliance_flags: UNESCO Convention on the Protection of the Underwater Cultural Heritage
